summary: Closed, unmerged tooling PR that introduced an initial Python script for parsing the generated GALE01.map link map and a Makefile hook named LMVIZ. The script tokenized map lines containing symbols, converted file-backed and linker-generated symbols into small dictionaries, appended them to a parsed list, and printed the count. The PR also changed GENERATE_MAP default from 0 to 1 and added .DS_Store to .gitignore. There was no PR body or review discussion in the provided slice, so rationale and reviewer intent are not evidenced.

key_files.role: New Python link map parsing prototype; reads build/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map and extracts symbol records from tokenized linker map lines.
Enabled map generation by default, declared LMVIZ := tools/linkmapviz, and added a build rule that invokes the Python script.

what_changed: Added tools/linkmapviz.py with comments documenting intended regexes for file-backed symbols and linker-generated symbols, though the implemented parser used whitespace tokenization rather than re matching.
Hard-coded the map input path as GALE01 = 'build/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map'.
Implemented tokens2obj(tokens) for six-token lines, returning a dictionary with level, id, ctype, scope, and objfile.
Implemented tokens2symbol(tokens) for seven-token linker-generated symbol lines, returning a dictionary with level, id, and ctype set to 'linkersymbol'.
Iterated through GALE01.map line by line, filtered for non-empty lines whose first token contains ']', parsed supported token counts, appended parsed dictionaries, and printed len(parsed).
Changed Makefile GENERATE_MAP default from 0 to 1, making link map generation the default behavior in this branch.
Added LMVIZ := tools/linkmapviz and a Makefile target that runs python3 on tools/linkmapviz.py.
smart_moves: The parser separated conversion of normal object-file symbols from linker-generated symbols into distinct helper functions, which would make later enrichment easier.
The script stored parsed rows as dictionaries with explicit fields such as level, id, ctype, scope, and objfile rather than leaving downstream logic to interpret raw token arrays.
Changing GENERATE_MAP to 1 ensured the expected GALE01.map input would be produced during normal builds in this experimental branch.

The diff documented the expected symbol line shapes in comments at the top of linkmapviz.py, providing useful breadcrumbs for future map parsing work even though the implementation did not yet use those regexes.
decomp_lessons: Link map tooling can support decomp work by exposing symbol/object-file relationships from GALE01.map, but parsers should avoid hard-coded build paths where possible and accept target/version paths as parameters.

When integrating analysis scripts into the Makefile, distinguish between a generated executable artifact and a script invocation; a target named tools/linkmapviz with no output file can be surprising if the source is tools/linkmapviz.py.
Defaulting GENERATE_MAP to 1 is useful for tools that consume maps, but it changes build behavior globally and should be intentional.
Whitespace token parsing may be enough for an early map parser prototype, but the commented regexes suggest the linker map format has identifiable structures that could be parsed more robustly.
For reusable decomp tooling, print-only prototypes should eventually emit structured output or visualization artifacts and document invocation.
naming_conventions: Makefile tool variables follow uppercase short names such as ELF2DOL, HOSTCC, SHA1SUM, PYTHON, and this PR added LMVIZ.
The hard-coded map constant was named GALE01, matching the generated GALE01.map game ID filename.
Parsed symbol dictionaries used concise keys: level, id, ctype, scope, and objfile.
Linker-generated symbols were classified with ctype value 'linkersymbol'.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching changes were present.
The tooling targeted linker map inspection, which can help future matching/decomp work by locating symbols, object files, symbol levels, C type categories, and scope metadata in GALE01.map.
The script inferred nesting or diagnostic severity from the raw level token by removing its trailing character and converting the remaining text to an integer.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided slice.
Because the PR was closed with merged_at null shortly after creation, it appears unmerged, but the slice does not provide a reason for closure.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs or commits adding a production link map visualization or parser tool.
Search for linkmapviz, LMVIZ, or tokens2obj to see whether this prototype was revived elsewhere.
Search for PRs changing GENERATE_MAP defaults or adding map-consuming tooling.
Look for review or issue discussion outside this PR explaining why the LinkMap Visualizer PR was closed unmerged.
